title: Dual Port Memory support for repl-seq-mem.
body:
I'm using the dual port SRAM in my project:
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 1.1.0
circuit RegFile :
  module RegFile :
    input clock : Clock
    output readPorts : { flip addr : UInt<7>, data : UInt<8>}[2]
    input writePort : { valid : UInt<1>, bits : { addr : UInt<7>, data : UInt<8>}}

    smem rf : UInt<8> [128]
    infer mport readPorts_0_data_MPORT = rf[readPorts[0].addr], clock
    readPorts[0].data <= readPorts_0_data_MPORT
    infer mport readPorts_1_data_MPORT = rf[readPorts[1].addr], clock
    readPorts[1].data <= readPorts_1_data_MPORT
    when writePort.valid :
      infer mport MPORT = rf[writePort.bits.addr], clock
      MPORT <= writePort.bits.data
```
It is supposed to be replaced to TSN28 Ultra High Density Dual Port SRAM in my design. The output should be smem, but I got combMem instead:
```
// Generated by CIRCT unknown git version
// Standard header to adapt well known macros to our needs.
`ifndef RANDOMIZE
  `ifdef RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
    `define RANDOMIZE
  `endif // RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
`endif // not def RANDOMIZE

// RANDOM may be set to an expression that produces a 32-bit random unsigned value.
`ifndef RANDOM
  `define RANDOM $random
`endif // not def RANDOM

// Users can define INIT_RANDOM as general code that gets injected into the
// initializer block for modules with registers.
`ifndef INIT_RANDOM
  `define INIT_RANDOM
`endif // not def INIT_RANDOM

// If using random initialization, you can also define RANDOMIZE_DELAY to
// customize the delay used, otherwise 0.002 is used.
`ifndef RANDOMIZE_DELAY
  `define RANDOMIZE_DELAY 0.002
`endif // not def RANDOMIZE_DELAY

// Define INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_ for use in our modules below.
`ifndef INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_
  `ifdef RANDOMIZE
    `ifdef VERILATOR
      `define INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_ `INIT_RANDOM
    `else  // VERILATOR
      `define INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_ `INIT_RANDOM #`RANDOMIZE_DELAY begin end
    `endif // VERILATOR
  `else  // RANDOMIZE
    `define INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_
  `endif // RANDOMIZE
`endif // not def INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_

// VCS coverage exclude_file
module rf_combMem(
  input  [6:0] R0_addr,
  input        R0_en,
               R0_clk,
  input  [6:0] R1_addr,
  input        R1_en,
               R1_clk,
  input  [6:0] W0_addr,
  input        W0_en,
               W0_clk,
  input  [7:0] W0_data,
  output [7:0] R0_data,
               R1_data
);

  reg [7:0] Memory[0:127];
  reg       _GEN;
  reg [6:0] _GEN_0;
  always @(posedge R0_clk) begin
    _GEN <= R0_en;
    _GEN_0 <= R0_addr;
  end // always @(posedge)
  reg       _GEN_1;
  reg [6:0] _GEN_2;
  always @(posedge R1_clk) begin
    _GEN_1 <= R1_en;
    _GEN_2 <= R1_addr;
  end // always @(posedge)
  always @(posedge W0_clk) begin
    if (W0_en)
      Memory[W0_addr] <= W0_data;
  end // always @(posedge)
  `ifndef SYNTHESIS
    `ifdef RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
      integer initvar;
      reg [31:0] _RANDOM_MEM;
    `endif // RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
    `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
      reg [31:0] _RANDOM;
    `endif // RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
    initial begin
      `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
        for (initvar = 0; initvar < 128; initvar = initvar + 1) begin
          _RANDOM_MEM = {`RANDOM};
          Memory[initvar] = _RANDOM_MEM[7:0];
        end
      `endif // RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
        _RANDOM = {`RANDOM};
        _GEN = _RANDOM[0];
        _GEN_0 = _RANDOM[7:1];
        _GEN_1 = _RANDOM[8];
        _GEN_2 = _RANDOM[15:9];
      `endif // RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
    end // initial
  `endif // not def SYNTHESIS
  assign R0_data = _GEN ? Memory[_GEN_0] : 8'bx;
  assign R1_data = _GEN_1 ? Memory[_GEN_2] : 8'bx;
endmodule

module RegFile(
  input        clock,
  input  [6:0] readPorts_0_addr,
               readPorts_1_addr,
  input        writePort_valid,
  input  [6:0] writePort_bits_addr,
  input  [7:0] writePort_bits_data,
  output [7:0] readPorts_0_data,
               readPorts_1_data
);

  rf_combMem rf_ext (
    .R0_addr (readPorts_0_addr),
    .R0_en   (1'h1),
    .R0_clk  (clock),
    .R1_addr (readPorts_1_addr),
    .R1_en   (1'h1),
    .R1_clk  (clock),
    .W0_addr (writePort_bits_addr),
    .W0_en   (writePort_valid),
    .W0_clk  (clock),
    .W0_data (writePort_bits_data),
    .R0_data (readPorts_0_data),
    .R1_data (readPorts_1_data)
  );
endmodule
```

I noticed there is a constraint in repl-seq-mem:
https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/846dd02bde760e39645643ce5f7fb9a26d89a38a/docs/Dialects/FIRRTL/RationaleFIRRTL.md?plain=1#L638-L647
I think it might be relevant.
